mechanism_narrative: Extremely low federal funds rate compresses discount rates, boosting equity valuations over the near term. The current rate of 0.07% is deeply accommodative and should support positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high unemployment indicates economic slack and weak consumer demand, which depresses corporate earnings and leads to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8%, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below a threshold, it signals a contraction in residential investment and broader economic weakness, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: If in periods when HOUST < 500 the S&P 500 20-day return is positive more than 40% of the time, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in nonfarm payrolls below a threshold signals imminent recessionary conditions, causing negative equity returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: If when PAYEMS < 130000 the S&P 500 5-day return is positive more than 40% of the time, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales and industrial production signals robust domestic demand and production, which together drive positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the combined signal (normalized sum of RSAFS and INDPRO) has a negative rank IC with 60-day forward returns over any rolling 2-year window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: An interaction of very low interest rates and high unemployment indicates that monetary policy is aggressively accommodative but failing to stimulate the labor market, which is a negative signal for equity markets as it suggests structural weakness.
failure_condition: If the interaction term (FEDFUNDS * UNRATE) has a positive rank IC with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: During a confirmed industrial expansion phase, rising industrial production signals capacity utilization and investment, which drive higher corporate earnings and positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: If during periods identified as industrial expansion (INDPRO > 95 and rising 3-month moving average), the S&P 500 60-day return is negative more than 50% of the time, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: In a consumer spending recovery regime, robust retail sales above a threshold confirm sustained consumption growth, which supports corporate revenues and leads to positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If when RSAFS > 350000 the S&P 500 20-day return is negative in more than 50% of instances, reject the hypothesis.
